core_mechanism: logarithmic potential theory in the plane: recovering a limiting two-dimensional probability measure as the Laplacian of the almost-sure limit of a logarithmic potential built from an associated spectrum, with control of the extreme (smallest) values of that spectrum to justify the limit near its singularity
brief_reason: The abstract proves convergence of an empirical point distribution to a uniform planar measure by applying potential theory in the complex plane and analyzing extreme singular values, i.e. reconstructing the limiting measure from its logarithmic potential while controlling the smallest singular values.
